blocks.text: 5660 W Flamingo Rd Ste B, Las Vegas, NV 89103-2312
Visited on: 2026 Apr 10 12:30 (Age at visit: 71 years)
Electronically signed by: JAYSON BAUTISTA, APRN on 2026-04-10 10:57
Initiation of Visit: This is a follow up which visit was initiated by the patient. The chief
MEDICAL NECESSITY: This visit with a FTF encounter with the patient in the home
is not a visit of convenience. This home visit is necessary to prevent exacerbations of
patient's ongoing medical condition. Patient is homebound, ambulates with hemicane,
with poor endurance and balance, unsteady gait, generalized muscle weakness, fatigues
easily, dyspnea with moderate exertion and consequently leaving home would require a
considerable and taxing effort. It is safer for patient to be seen in their own environment in
Patient, Robert Bryant is an 71 year-old African American male, alert and oriented x4.
He lives with roommates, in a 2 story home. Patient is being seen today with complaint
of palpitations. Patient reports noticing palpitations the last few weeks and he correlates
it when he is taking the nifedipine. Patient stopped nifedipine and palpitations would go
away. He is wondering if its related. Patient instructed that nifedipine can cause reflex
tachycardia. He denies any chest pain or shortness of breath. He has no blood pressure
machine to check his vitals, will attempts to get him one through his medicaid. Patient
established with RTC, but will hold off on outpatient therapy till we settle on the palpitations.
Will order chest x-ray and ekg, will discontinue nifedipine for now. Skilled nursing is
needed for monitoring vital signs, teachings on disease processes, home safety, education
on medication use and side effects. With patient not being able to attend outpatient
therapy, patient will benefit with occupational therapy to continue working on upper body
strengthening and conditioning. Patient has PMHx CVA with right-sided hemiparesis,
constipation, bilateral testicular microlithiasis, and multiple left epididymal cysts. Surgical
This visit was conducted following strict infection control measures as per CDC guidelines.
Patient is homebound due to poor endurance and balance, unsteady gait, weakness,
fatigues easily, dyspnea with moderate exertion and requires a taxing effort to leave home.
Uses motorized wheelchair for long distances
Constitutional: No significant changes in weight. Denies fever, chills.
HEENT: Denies eye pain. Reports blurred vision. Denies hearing problem. No odynophagia
or dysphagia. No lumps in neck. No epistaxis. No hemoptysis.
Cardiac: Denies chest pain. Denies swelling. Reports palpitations after taking nifedipine
Respiratory: Denies hemoptysis, wheeze. Reports short of breath with moderate exertion.
GI: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea. Denies melena, hematochezia. Denies abdominal
pain. Reports good appetite. Reports constipation

The patient's speech was normal, sharing conversation with normal laryngeal efforts.
Appropriate mood and affect were seen on exam. Thought processes were logical,
relevant, and thoughts were completed normally. Thought content was normal. Thought
content was normal with no psychotic or suicidal thoughts. The patient's judgement was
realistic with normal insight into their present condition. Mental status included: correct
time, place, person orientation, normal recent and remote memory, normal attention span
and concentration ability. Language skills included the ability to correctly name objects.
Knowledge included normal awareness of current and past events.

handrails on the stairs or have poor lighting? Yes

- It has been explained to Patient that the Nurse Practitioner and the care plan is being
supervised by a Physician - Pt/CG verbalized understanding.
-Total encounter time with patient was more than 35 minutes which includes history taking,
physical examination, medication reconciliation and discussion of treatment plan. Chart
reviewed for 15 minutes prior to this visit. Chart documentation after visit 30 minutes.
- Financial, privacy, and consent to treat notices are signed and understood by patient/CG.
- It was explained to patient/CG that for a provider to visit and treat patient in the home,
patient needs to qualify and fit the criteria for transitional care and homebound status. If
patient is able to to see their PCP in the office, returns to baseline, and/or no longer fits the
criteria, the homebound status and the transitional care period ends.
- It was explained to the patient/CG that the patient is permitted to have only one PCP
or transitional care/gap provider at any given time. If the patient has more than one PCP
or transitional care provider at any given time, insurance may not cover and patient is
- Recommend Home Health Services to prevent injuries to self, progression of diseases,
SN to monitor, assess, and for progression of disease.
SN to evaluate knowledge deficits of Pt and family regarding disease processes,
medications, medication regimen, comorbidities, and care plan and educate appropriately.
SN to take VS and log into book for data and history. Report for BP <90/60 or >160/90,
HR <60 or >100, RR <14 or >20, and T <96.0 or >101. Blood glucose <80 or >300
SN needed d/t high potential for disease progression and inpatient hospitalizations.
SN to evaluate patient's knowledge deficit and teach accordingly the disease process and
management of unstable medical conditions including comorbid factors.
Report s/s of infection or abnormal wound conditions.
Pt needs assistance with medication management, observation and assessment for
Pt will need skilled nursing due to high potential for hospital admission.
Pt will need skilled nursing for teachings of disease process, diet and new medication
PT/OT to evaluate and treat appropriately to increase functionality, decreases debility and

perform ADL's safely and independently. Patient is to improve and/or return to baseline.
